title: Why Network as a Service (NaaS) is the Future for Distributed Enterprises
summary: NaaS is a cloud delivery model that replaces traditional networking with subscription-based, software-defined infrastructure, enabling enterprises to scale globally with lower upfront costs and faster deployment. The shift from hardware-heavy, manual networking to automated, centralized management via software offers instant elasticity and integrated security features.
content:

The traditional perimeter-based network is dead. As enterprises shift to
For modern businesses, the "office" is no longer a centralized location; it is
Traditional networking models, reliant on physical hardware, manual

Software-Defined Networking (SDN): Decouples the control plane from the data plane, allowing for centralized management via software.
Automated Provisioning: New sites or users can be onboarded in minutes, not months.
Global Reach: Leverage the provider’s infrastructure to connect geographically dispersed locations seamlessly.

zh.title: 为什么网络即服务(NaaS)是分布式企业的未来
zh.summary: NaaS是一种云交付模式，用基于订阅的软件定义基础设施替代传统网络，使企业能够以更低的前期成本和更快的部署速度全球扩展。从硬件密集、手动网络向通过软件自动化、集中管理的转变提供了即时弹性和集成安全功能。

fr.title: Pourquoi Network as a Service (NaaS) est l'avenir des entreprises distribuées
fr.summary: NaaS est un modèle de distribution cloud qui remplace la mise en réseau traditionnelle par une infrastructure définie par logiciel basée sur l'abonnement, permettant aux entreprises de se développer à l'échelle mondiale avec des coûts initiaux plus faibles et un déploiement plus rapide. Le passage de réseaux lourds et manuels à une gestion automatisée et centralisée par logiciel offre une élasticité instantanée et des fonctionnalités de sécurité intégrées.

de.title: Warum Network as a Service (NaaS) die Zukunft für verteilte Unternehmen ist
de.summary: NaaS ist ein Cloud-Bereitstellungsmodell, das traditionelle Netzwerke durch abonnementbasierte, softwaregesteuerte Infrastruktur ersetzt und es Unternehmen ermöglicht, global mit niedrigeren Anfangskosten und schnellerer Bereitstellung zu skalieren. Die Umstellung von hardwareintensiven, manuellen Netzwerken auf automatisierte, zentral verwaltete Software bietet sofortige Elastizität und integrierte Sicherheitsfunktionen.

es.title: Por qué Network as a Service (NaaS) es el futuro de las empresas distribuidas
es.summary: NaaS es un modelo de entrega en la nube que reemplaza la red tradicional con infraestructura basada en suscripción definida por software, permitiendo a las empresas escalar globalmente con menores costos iniciales e implementación más rápida. El cambio de redes pesadas y manuales a gestión automatizada y centralizada por software ofrece elasticidad instantánea e funciones de seguridad integradas.

title: Day 15 – Building Your First Simple AI Agent
summary: This guide builds AI agents through four essential components—an LLM for reasoning, tools for action, memory for context, and a control loop for decision-making—emphasizing well-defined goals and a think-act-observe cycle. The structured approach teaches agents to plan efficiently, use tools autonomously, and recognize when goals are achieved, applicable to any agent implementation.
content:
From Theory to Reality


So far, you’ve learned what agents are, how they think, and when to use them.
Now comes the turning point:
Let’s build one — conceptually, step by step.
No frameworks yet.

No code yet.
Just a clean mental model you can apply anywhere.
🎯 Goal: Create an AI agent that can
Investigate a user question, gather information, and return a reasoned answer.
Example task:
“Find the top 3 risks of deploying agentic AI in production and summarize them.”
This task requires:
understanding intent

deciding steps

using tools

stopping at the right time



Perfect for a first agent.
Bad agent goal ❌
“Be a research assistant.”
Good agent goal ✅
“Given a question, search trusted sources, summarize findings, and return a concise answer.”
Agents without clear scope:
wander

overthink

rack up cost



🎯 Rule: One agent = one responsibility.
Every agent needs four building blocks:



Component
Purpose




LLM
Reasoning + language


Tools
Act on the world


Memory
Track context


Control Loop
Decide what to do next



Let’s assemble them.
This is the heart of an agent.
Think → Act → Observe → Decide
Visualized:
User Goal
   ↓
Reason
   ↓
Use Tool
   ↓
Observe Result
   ↓
Is Goal Done?
   ├─ No → Reason Again
   └─ Yes → Respond

This loop is what separates agents from single-shot prompts.
Before acting, the agent asks:
What information do I need?
Which tools can help?
What’s the shortest path?
Example plan:
Search for “agentic AI production risks”

Extract recurring themes

Rank top 3

Summarize clearly



🧠 This is implicit planning — no fancy planner required yet.
Tools are how agents leave the chat.
For our agent:



Tool
Why Needed




Web search
Gather info


Document reader
Parse content


Notes store
Track findings



The agent decides:
“I don’t know enough — I need to search.”
That decision is autonomy.
At minimum, the agent remembers:
the original goal

what it already tried

key findings




  
  
  Types of memory used here



Memory Type
Example

zh.title: 第15天 - 构建你的第一个简单AI代理
zh.summary: 本指南通过四个基本组件构建AI代理——用于推理的LLM、用于行动的工具、用于上下文的记忆和用于决策的控制循环——强调明确定义的目标和"思考-行动-观察"循环。这种结构化方法教授代理高效规划、自主使用工具，并识别目标何时达成，适用于任何代理实现。

fr.title: Jour 15 - Créer votre premier agent IA simple
fr.summary: Ce guide construit des agents IA en utilisant quatre composants essentiels - un LLM pour le raisonnement, des outils pour l'action, la mémoire pour le contexte et une boucle de contrôle pour la prise de décision - en mettant l'accent sur des objectifs bien définis et un cycle "penser-agir-observer". L'approche structurée enseigne aux agents de planifier efficacement, d'utiliser les outils de manière autonome et de reconnaître quand les objectifs sont atteints, applicable à toute implémentation d'agent.

de.title: Tag 15 – Erstellen Sie Ihren ersten einfachen KI-Agenten
de.summary: Dieser Leitfaden erstellt KI-Agenten durch vier wesentliche Komponenten – ein LLM zum Denken, Werkzeuge zum Handeln, Speicher für Kontext und eine Kontrollschleife zur Entscheidungsfindung – mit Betonung auf klar definierte Ziele und einen "Denk-Handlungs-Beobachtungs"-Zyklus. Der strukturierte Ansatz lehrt Agenten, effizient zu planen, Werkzeuge autonom zu nutzen und zu erkennen, wann Ziele erreicht sind, anwendbar auf jede Agent-Implementierung.

es.title: Día 15 - Construir su primer agente IA simple
es.summary: Esta guía construye agentes IA a través de cuatro componentes esenciales: un LLM para el razonamiento, herramientas para la acción, memoria para el contexto y un ciclo de control para la toma de decisiones, enfatizando objetivos bien definidos y un ciclo "pensar-actuar-observar". El enfoque estructurado enseña a los agentes a planificar eficientemente, usar herramientas de forma autónoma y reconocer cuándo se logran los objetivos, aplicable a cualquier implementación de agentes.

title: How I Run a Team of AI Coding Agents in Parallel
summary: Running multiple AI coding agents in parallel creates file collisions, untested merges, and context gaps—problems solved by Batty, a terminal-based supervisor that coordinates agents through hierarchical roles (architect, manager, engineers) in isolated environments. Automated kanban tracking and test gates ensure code integrity while enabling productive multi-agent workflows.
content:
Running one AI coding agent is productive. Running five in parallel is chaos.
I've been using Claude Code daily for months. It's great — until you realize there are four other tasks sitting idle while you wait for one agent to finish refactoring a module. So you open more terminal tabs, spin up more sessions, and now you're the bottleneck. You're context-switching between agents, resolving merge conflicts they created, and manually checking if anything still compiles.
I spent a week in this mode before I decided there had to be a better way.
Here's what goes wrong when you naively run multiple AI coding agents on the same repo:
They stomp on each other's files. Agent A edits src/auth.rs while Agent B is also editing src/auth.rs. Someone loses.
Nobody checks the tests. An agent says "Done!" but the test suite is failing. You don't find out until three more tasks are stacked on top of the broken one.
You become the dispatcher. Which agent is working on what? Is anyone idle? Did that task actually get assigned? You're doing more coordination than coding.
There's no shared context. Agent A doesn't know Agent B just changed the API interface it depends on. Chaos.
Sound familiar? If you're using Claude Code, Codex, or Aider, and you've ever wanted to run more than one at a time — this is the wall you hit.
I built Batty — a terminal-native supervisor that turns multiple AI coding agents into a coordinated team. No web UI. No servers. Just your terminal and tmux.
The core idea: instead of a flat pool of agents, you define a hierarchy. An architect agent plans the work. A manager breaks it into tasks. Engineers execute in isolated environments. A kanban board tracks everything. Tests gate completion.
Here's the minimal setup — an architect and three engineers:
# .batty/team_config/team.yaml
name: my-project
board:
  rotation_threshold: 20
standup:
  interval_secs: 600
  output_lines: 40
roles:
  - name: architect
    role_type: architect
    agent: claude
    instances: 1

zh.title: 我如何并行运行一个AI编码代理团队
zh.summary: 并行运行多个AI编码代理会造成文件冲突、未测试的合并和上下文缺口——这些问题通过Batty解决，Batty是一个基于终端的主管程序，通过分层角色（架构师、经理、工程师）在隔离环境中协调代理。自动化看板跟踪和测试门确保代码完整性，同时实现高效的多代理工作流。

fr.title: Comment je gère une équipe d'agents de codage IA en parallèle
fr.summary: Exécuter plusieurs agents de codage IA en parallèle crée des collisions de fichiers, des fusions non testées et des lacunes de contexte—des problèmes résolus par Batty, un superviseur basé sur terminal qui coordonne les agents à travers des rôles hiérarchiques (architecte, manager, ingénieurs) dans des environnements isolés. Le suivi des kanban automatisé et les portes de test garantissent l'intégrité du code tout en permettant des workflows multi-agents productifs.

de.title: Wie ich ein Team von KI-Codierungsagenten parallel ausführe
de.summary: Das parallele Ausführen mehrerer KI-Codierungsagenten erzeugt Dateikollisionen, ungetestete Zusammenführungen und Kontextlücken—Probleme, die durch Batty gelöst werden, einen terminalgestützten Supervisor, der Agenten durch hierarchische Rollen (Architekt, Manager, Ingenieure) in isolierten Umgebungen koordiniert. Automatisierte Kanban-Verfolgung und Test-Gates stellen die Codeintegrität sicher und ermöglichen gleichzeitig produktive Multi-Agent-Workflows.

es.title: Cómo ejecuto un equipo de agentes de codificación de IA en paralelo
es.summary: Ejecutar múltiples agentes de codificación de IA en paralelo crea colisiones de archivos, fusiones no probadas y brechas de contexto—problemas resueltos por Batty, un supervisor basado en terminal que coordina agentes a través de roles jerárquicos (arquitecto, gerente, ingenieros) en ambientes aislados. El seguimiento automático de kanban y las compuertas de prueba garantizan la integridad del código mientras permiten flujos de trabajo multi-agentes productivos.

title: 고양이 떨어지는 원리 과학적 설명과 안전한 수직공간 활용법
summary: Cats' famous landing ability relies on the righting reflex—a biomechanical process involving spine flexibility that enables mid-fall reorientation, first formally studied in 1984. Understanding this reflex helps pet owners design safe vertical spaces protecting cats' spines and joints, recognizing that while cats are naturally agile, their physical limits require thoughtful environmental design.
content:
🐾 The Righting Reflex: Science Behind Cat Falls & Safe Vertical Spaces


⚠️ Medical Disclaimer: This content is for general informational purposes only and does not replace professional veterinary diagnosis or treatment. If your cat has health concerns, please consult a veterinarian.
As a cat owner, you’ve likely witnessed the uncanny ability of your feline friend to land on their feet, no matter how high they fall. It’s a mesmerizing display of biology that often feels like magic. However, for cat care experts and home safety enthusiasts, this ability isn't just a party trick—it’s a critical factor in designing a safe home environment.
This article is brought to you by PlayCat Research (playcat.xyz), a Korean cat behavioral enrichment project dedicated to understanding feline needs through science.
While cats are naturally agile, their physical limits are real. This guide is based on scientific research into the "Falling Cat Problem" to explain how to construct safe vertical spaces. By understanding the biomechanics of a cat's fall, you can design your home to protect their spine and joints, ensuring they stay healthy and happy.

Many pet owners wonder, "How does a cat know which way is down?" This ability, known as the Righting Reflex, is the foundation of home safety. In 1984, French animal behavior researchers formally raised the "Falling Cat Problem" to study the mechanics behind this reflex.
To understand the limits of this ability, the research team conducted rigorous experiments. In the first phase, they measured the strength and rotation of five cat corpses by bending and twisting their spines. This method allowed researchers to gather biomechanical data without harming living animals. The results were definitive: spine flexibility is the core element that enables rotation in mid-air.
The study revealed that cats separate their front and back bodies to regulate rotation speed during a fall. If the front rotates first, the back rotates in the opposite direc

zh.title: 猫咪翻身反射的科学原理与安全垂直空间设计
zh.summary: 猫咪著名的着陆能力源于翻身反射——这是一种涉及脊椎灵活性的生物力学过程，能够在坠落中进行重新定向，最早在1984年被正式研究。理解这种反射有助于宠物主人设计安全的垂直空间，保护猫咪的脊椎和关节，认识到虽然猫咪天生敏捷，但它们的身体极限需要周到的环保设计。

fr.title: La science du réflexe d'équilibre du chat et la conception d'espaces verticaux sûrs
fr.summary: La célèbre capacité d'atterrissage des chats repose sur le réflexe d'équilibre - un processus biomécanique impliquant la flexibilité de la colonne vertébrale qui permet la réorientation en chute libre, formellement étudié pour la première fois en 1984. Comprendre ce réflexe aide les propriétaires d'animaux à concevoir des espaces verticaux sûrs protégeant les colonnes vertébrales et les articulations des chats, reconnaissant que bien que les chats soient naturellement agiles, leurs limites physiques nécessitent une conception environnementale réfléchie.

de.title: Die Wissenschaft des Selbstaufrichtungsreflexes der Katze und sichere vertikale Raumgestaltung
de.summary: Die berühmte Landefähigkeit von Katzen beruht auf dem Selbstaufrichtungsreflex – einem biomechanischen Prozess, bei dem die Wirbelsäulenflexibilität eine Umorientiertung während des Sturzes ermöglicht und 1984 erstmals formal untersucht wurde. Das Verständnis dieses Reflexes hilft Haustierbesitzern, sichere vertikale Räume zu gestalten, die die Wirbelsäule und Gelenke von Katzen schützen, und erkennt an, dass Katzen zwar von Natur aus wendig sind, ihre körperlichen Grenzen aber eine durchdachte Umgebungsgestaltung erfordern.

es.title: La ciencia del reflejo de enderezamiento del gato y el diseño de espacios verticales seguros
es.summary: La famosa capacidad de aterrizaje de los gatos se basa en el reflejo de enderezamiento, un proceso biomecánico que implica la flexibilidad de la columna vertebral que permite la reorientación durante la caída, estudiado formalmente por primera vez en 1984. Comprender este reflejo ayuda a los dueños de mascotas a diseñar espacios verticales seguros que protejan la columna vertebral y las articulaciones de los gatos, reconociendo que aunque los gatos son naturalmente ágiles, sus límites físicos requieren un diseño ambiental reflexivo.

title: What I Learned Automating Software Development (After 20 Years of Doing It Manually)
summary: Building an entire product (OpenLoop) through 160+ emails to an AI agent revealed that while AI excels at fast scaffolding and following explicit instructions, it fails at end-to-end testing and maintaining long-conversation context. The AI successfully implemented standard patterns like auth flows but missed functional issues invisible from the server side, highlighting the critical gap between successful builds and working applications.
content:
In Part 1, I told the story of building OpenLoop — an open-source feedback platform — by emailing an AI agent for 5 days. 160+ emails, $15 in tokens, zero lines of human-written code, and a working product at the end.
Now let's talk about what I actually learned.

Let's start with the positive, because there's plenty of it.
Scaffolding speed is unreal. Within 90 minutes of the first email, the AI had a working Astro + React + Tailwind project, a Supabase schema with six tables and row-level security, a feedback widget, public roadmap and announcements pages, and an admin dashboard skeleton. That's not a weekend project — that's a weekend project done before my coffee got cold.
It follows explicit instructions well. "No Next.js, go Astro" — done. "Name is OpenLoop" — rebranded everything. "Widget takes a userId, not email" — refactored. When you're clear about what you want, the AI delivers. The problems start when there's ambiguity, but that's true of any team member, human or android.
Common patterns: Auth flows, RLS policies, webhook handlers — it knows how these are supposed to look. You don't explain what a protected route is. You just say "add auth to the admin panel" and it does it correctly.
Research: When it hit something unfamiliar it would go find a working examples and documentation, read how that library expected to be used, and implement it. Not hallucinate an API. We have come a long way since the first versions of Github Copilot with GPT 3
Equally important to be honest about.
"It builds" is not "it works." This was the single biggest recurring issue. The AI would run npm run build, see it pass, and declare the job done. But a successful build tells you nothing about whether a human can actually use the thing. Buttons that don't do anything, pages that render blank, widgets nested inside widgets — the AI couldn't see any of that. It was testing from the server's perspective, never from the user's chair.
Context amnesia is brutal. The conversation hit

zh.title: 我在自动化软件开发中学到的东西（在手动开发了20年之后）
zh.summary: 通过超过160封电子邮件向AI代理构建整个产品（OpenLoop）揭示了虽然AI擅长快速搭建和遵循明确指令，但在端到端测试和维护长对话上下文方面失败。AI成功实现了如认证流程等标准模式，但错过了从服务器端看不见的功能问题，突显了成功构建和工作应用程序之间的关键差距。

fr.title: Ce que j'ai appris en automatisant le développement logiciel (après 20 ans de le faire manuellement)
fr.summary: La construction d'un produit entier (OpenLoop) via plus de 160 emails à un agent IA a révélé que bien que l'IA excelle dans l'échafaudage rapide et le suivi des instructions explicites, elle échoue dans les tests de bout en bout et le maintien du contexte de longue conversation. L'IA a mis en œuvre avec succès des modèles standard comme les flux d'authentification mais a manqué les problèmes fonctionnels invisibles du côté serveur, mettant en évidence l'écart critique entre les builds réussis et les applications fonctionnelles.

de.title: Was ich beim Automatisieren von Softwareentwicklung gelernt habe (nach 20 Jahren manueller Entwicklung)
de.summary: Die Entwicklung eines gesamten Produkts (OpenLoop) durch über 160 E-Mails an einen KI-Agenten offenbarte, dass während KI bei schnellem Gerüstbau und dem Befolgen expliziter Anweisungen hervorragend ist, sie bei End-to-End-Tests und der Aufrechterhaltung des Kontexts langer Konversationen scheitert. Die KI implementierte erfolgreich Standardmuster wie Authentifizierungsflüsse, übersah aber funktionale Probleme, die von der Serverseite unsichtbar sind, und hebt die kritische Lücke zwischen erfolgreichen Builds und funktionierenden Anwendungen hervor.

es.title: Lo que aprendí automatizando el desarrollo de software (después de 20 años haciéndolo manualmente)
es.summary: Construir un producto completo (OpenLoop) a través de más de 160 correos electrónicos a un agente de IA reveló que aunque la IA sobresale en andamios rápidos y seguir instrucciones explícitas, falla en pruebas de extremo a extremo y en mantener el contexto de conversación larga. La IA implementó con éxito patrones estándar como flujos de autenticación pero perdió problemas funcionales invisibles desde el lado del servidor, destacando la brecha crítica entre compilaciones exitosas y aplicaciones que funcionan.

title: The Pre-Flight Checklist: 9 Things to Analyze Before Cutting Any AWS Cost
summary: Before optimizing AWS costs, conduct comprehensive analysis of traffic patterns, SLO status, cache dependencies, and incident history to protect system reliability. Premature optimization (like the case study's ill-timed RDS downsize causing a 90-minute outage) underscores why understanding workload patterns and peak times is essential before any cost-cutting decision.
content:
The Pre-Flight Checklist: 9 Things to Analyze Before Cutting Any AWS Cost


Tags: aws finops sre reliability devops
Last month I saved $12K/year by cleaning up AWS waste across four accounts. But before I touched a single resource, I spent two days just analyzing. Not because I'm cautious by nature — because I've seen what happens when people skip this step.
A colleague at a previous company followed AWS Cost Explorer's recommendation to downsize an RDS instance. It was 12% CPU average — seemed obvious. What they didn't check: that instance handled a 4x traffic spike every Friday at 6 PM. The downsize turned Friday evening into a 90-minute outage, a rollback, and an incident report that took longer to write than the analysis would have.
The rule: never optimize what you don't fully understand.
This article is the pre-flight checklist I run before every cost optimization. It's conversational by design — I want you to internalize the thinking, not just memorize a checklist.
The SRE Guarantee: Before any optimization, we guarantee error budget protection, assured minimum downtime, and reliability over savings. See the series introduction for the full guarantee. Every check in this article enforces that guarantee.
Automate this: finops preflight runs this entire analysis from your terminal.
aws-finops-toolkit.
The first question isn't "what does this cost?" — it's "what does this do?"
What to pull:
Current TPS / QPS (transactions or queries per second)
Peak QPS over the last 30 days
When the peak happens (time of day, day of week)
How I check it:
# ALB request count — last 7 days, 1-hour intervals
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
  --namespace AWS/ApplicationELB \
  --metric-name RequestCount \
  --dimensions Name=LoadBalancer,Value=app/pn-sh-alb/abc123 \
  --start-time $(date -u -v-7d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S) \
  --end-time $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S) \
  --period 3600 \
  --statistics Sum \
  --profile dodo-dev \
  --region ap-northeast-2

# RDS connections — peak

zh.title: 飞行前检查清单：削减任何AWS成本前要分析的9件事
zh.summary: 在优化AWS成本之前，进行流量模式、SLO状态、缓存依赖项和事件历史的全面分析，以保护系统可靠性。过早优化（如案例研究中不合时宜的RDS缩小导致90分钟中断）强调了在进行任何成本削减决策之前理解工作负载模式和高峰时间的重要性。

fr.title: Liste de contrôle pré-vol : 9 choses à analyser avant de réduire les coûts AWS
fr.summary: Avant d'optimiser les coûts AWS, menez une analyse complète des modèles de trafic, du statut SLO, des dépendances en cache et de l'historique des incidents pour protéger la fiabilité du système. L'optimisation prématurée (comme la réduction RDS mal synchronisée de l'étude de cas causant une panne de 90 minutes) souligne pourquoi la compréhension des modèles de charge de travail et des heures de pointe est essentielle avant toute décision de réduction des coûts.

de.title: Vorflug-Checkliste: 9 Dinge zu analysieren, bevor man AWS-Kosten senkt
de.summary: Führen Sie vor der Optimierung der AWS-Kosten eine umfassende Analyse von Datenverkehrsmustern, SLO-Status, Cache-Abhängigkeiten und Vorfallhistorie durch, um die Systemzuverlässigkeit zu schützen. Vorzeitige Optimierung (wie die schlecht zeitgesteuerte RDS-Verkleinerung in der Fallstudie, die zu einem 90-minütigen Ausfall führte) unterstreicht, warum das Verständnis von Arbeitslastmustern und Spitzenlastzeiten vor jeder Kostensenkungsentscheidung wesentlich ist.

es.title: Lista de verificación previa al vuelo: 9 cosas a analizar antes de reducir cualquier costo de AWS
es.summary: Antes de optimizar los costos de AWS, realice un análisis exhaustivo de patrones de tráfico, estado de SLO, dependencias de caché e historial de incidentes para proteger la confiabilidad del sistema. La optimización prematura (como el cambio de tamaño de RDS mal sincronizado del estudio de caso que causó una interrupción de 90 minutos) subraya por qué comprender los patrones de carga de trabajo y los tiempos máximos es esencial antes de cualquier decisión de reducción de costos.

title: FinOps for SREs: Cutting Costs Without Breaking Things
summary: SRE-driven FinOps prioritizes reliability over aggressive cost cutting by guaranteeing error budget protection and minimum downtime before optimization begins. This methodology identified $48-67K/year in potential savings across four AWS accounts while maintaining production stability, encoding reliability constraints into cost optimization tools rather than pursuing savings at any risk.
content:
FinOps for SREs: Cutting Costs Without Breaking Things


Tags: aws finops sre reliability devops
Most FinOps advice starts with a cost dashboard. This series starts with a different question: how do we cut costs without violating our SLOs?
I'm an SRE at a subsidiary of one of Korea's largest tech companies, managing four AWS accounts connected via a Transit Gateway hub-spoke architecture. When I was asked to reduce cloud spend, I didn't open AWS Cost Explorer first. I opened our SigNoz dashboards and checked our error budgets.
That's the difference between FinOps and SRE-driven FinOps.
Before any cost optimization begins, I guarantee three things:
1. Error Budget Protection
2. Assured Minimum Downtime
3. Reliability Over Savings
This guarantee isn't just a principle. It's encoded in every check of the aws-finops-toolkit — the open-source CLI I built to automate this workflow.
This series walks through the complete FinOps workflow I used to identify $48-67K/year in savings across four AWS accounts — starting with analysis, through passive cleanup, to active downsizing with SRE guardrails.
Part 0: The Pre-Flight Checklist


9 checks before cutting any cost. Traffic analysis, SLO status, cache dependencies, incident history, RI/SP coverage, and more. This is the analysis phase — never optimize what you don't fully understand.
→ OSS: finops preflight command (aws-finops-toolkit)
Part 1: How I Found $12K/Year in AWS Waste


Passive waste — things nobody uses. Abandoned VPCs ($748/mo), orphan CloudWatch log groups ($110-165/mo), S3 lifecycle vs Intelligent-Tiering ($75-104/mo). Zero risk to production. Total: $933-1,017/month.
→ OSS: finops scan — vpc_waste, cloudwatch_waste, s3_lifecycle checks
Part 2: Downsizing Without Downtime


Active optimization — shrinking running infrastructure with SRE guardrails. EC2/EKS right-sizing with PDBs, NAT Gateway replacement, Spot with drain handlers, RDS right-sizing with read replicas and cold cache planning, ElastiCache scheduling,

zh.title: SRE的FinOps：降低成本而不破坏系统
zh.summary: SRE驱动的FinOps通过保证错误预算保护和优化前的最小停机时间，优先考虑可靠性而不是积极削减成本。该方法在四个AWS账户中发现了每年48-67K美元的潜在节省，同时保持生产稳定性，将可靠性约束编码到成本优化工具中，而不是不顾一切地追求节省。

fr.title: FinOps pour les SRE : Réduire les coûts sans rien casser
fr.summary: FinOps piloté par les SRE priorise la fiabilité plutôt que la réduction agressive des coûts en garantissant la protection du budget d'erreur et les temps d'arrêt minimum avant optimisation. Cette méthodologie a identifié des économies potentielles de 48-67 K$/an sur quatre comptes AWS tout en maintenant la stabilité de la production, codant les contraintes de fiabilité dans les outils d'optimisation des coûts plutôt que de rechercher des économies à tout prix.

de.title: FinOps für SREs: Kosten senken ohne etwas zu beschädigen
de.summary: SRE-getriebenes FinOps priorisiert Zuverlässigkeit gegenüber aggressivem Kostenabbau durch Garantie des Fehlerhaushaltsschutzes und minimaler Ausfallzeiten vor Optimierung. Diese Methodik identifizierte 48-67 K$/Jahr potenzielle Einsparungen über vier AWS-Konten hinweg, während die Produktionsstabilität erhalten blieb, und kodierte Zuverlässigkeitsbeschränkungen in Kostenoptimierungstools anstelle der Verfolgung von Einsparungen um jeden Preis.

es.title: FinOps para SREs: Reducir costos sin romper nada
es.summary: FinOps impulsado por SRE prioriza la confiabilidad sobre la reducción agresiva de costos al garantizar la protección del presupuesto de errores y el tiempo de inactividad mínimo antes de la optimización. Esta metodología identificó ahorros potenciales de $48-67K/año en cuatro cuentas de AWS mientras mantenía la estabilidad de producción, codificando restricciones de confiabilidad en herramientas de optimización de costos en lugar de buscar ahorros a cualquier costo.

title: How I Found $12K/Year in AWS Waste Across 4 Accounts — Without Touching Production
summary: Through systematic analysis of a multi-account AWS architecture, an SRE identified $644/month in immediate waste (orphaned resources, unused WorkSpaces, log groups) and $933-1,017/month in total savings annually without modifying production services. The transparent, multi-account cost allocation by business unit demonstrates high-ROI passive cleanup with zero production risk.
content:
How I Found $12K/Year in AWS Waste Across 4 Accounts — Without Touching Production


Tags: aws finops cloudcost sre
I joined a subsidiary of one of Korea's largest tech companies at the beginning of 2026 as the sole SRE. I inherited four AWS accounts — hub, shared, waitlist, and loyalty — connected via a Transit Gateway hub-spoke architecture. Each account ran its own mix of EKS clusters, RDS instances, Aurora clusters, legacy EC2 services, and networking stacks accumulated over several years by multiple teams.
Nobody had done a cost audit since the accounts were created. Resources from decommissioned projects were still running. Log groups from deleted infrastructure were still ingesting. Six WorkSpaces that nobody had logged into for months were quietly billing $525/month.
Within two weeks of part-time analysis and execution, I cut $644/month in immediate waste and identified a total of $933-1,017/month ($11.2-12.2K/year) across three workstreams — all without touching a single production service. Beyond that, I mapped out a P0-P2 roadmap worth $48-67K/year that is now pending platform team approval.
This is what the work actually looked like.
Before optimizing anything, I needed to understand what we were paying for and who owned it. Our four accounts mapped to distinct business units:



Account
What runs there




hub
Transit Gateway, ArgoCD, ECR, bastion, monitoring (SigNoz)


shared
Ordering platform (EKS, RDS, microservices)


waitlist
Waitlist service (Aurora clusters, legacy EC2)


loyalty
Loyalty platform (RDS, Aurora, legacy WorkSpaces, VPN)



Each account gets its own AWS bill, which is one of the underappreciated benefits of multi-account architecture for FinOps. No tagging allocation formulas, no arguments about which team caused a cost spike. The Transit Gateway attachment cost ($51.10/month per VPC) is the "tax" each spoke pays for connectivity — and it is transparent.
I added standardized cost allocation tags derived from our existing Terraform na

zh.title: 我如何在4个AWS账户中发现每年12K美元的浪费——不涉及生产环境
zh.summary: 通过对多账户AWS架构的系统分析，一名SRE识别了每月644美元的直接浪费（孤立资源、未使用的工作空间、日志组）以及每年933-1,017美元的总节省，而无需修改生产服务。透明的按业务单位进行的多账户成本分配展示了零生产风险的高ROI被动清理。

fr.title: Comment j'ai trouvé 12K$/an de gaspillage AWS sur 4 comptes — Sans toucher à la production
fr.summary: Grâce à une analyse systématique d'une architecture AWS multi-comptes, un SRE a identifié 644$/mois de gaspillage immédiat (ressources orphelines, WorkSpaces inutilisés, groupes de journaux) et 933-1 017$/mois d'économies totales annuelles sans modifier les services de production. L'allocation transparente des coûts multi-comptes par unité commerciale démontre un nettoyage passif à haut ROI avec zéro risque de production.

de.title: Wie ich 12K$/Jahr AWS-Verschwendung über 4 Konten gefunden habe — Ohne die Produktion zu berühren
de.summary: Durch systematische Analyse einer Multi-Account-AWS-Architektur identifizierte ein SRE sofort 644$/Monat an Verschwendung (verwaiste Ressourcen, ungenutzte WorkSpaces, Protokollgruppen) und 933-1.017$/Monat an Gesamteinsparungen pro Jahr, ohne Produktionsservices zu ändern. Die transparente, kontenübergreifende Kostenzuteilung nach Geschäftseinheit demonstriert hochrentable passive Bereinigung mit null Produktionsrisiko.

es.title: Cómo encontré $12K/año en desperdicio de AWS en 4 cuentas — Sin tocar la producción
es.summary: A través del análisis sistemático de una arquitectura AWS de múltiples cuentas, un SRE identificó $644/mes en desperdicio inmediato (recursos huérfanos, WorkSpaces no utilizados, grupos de registros) y $933-1.017/mes en ahorros totales anuales sin modificar los servicios de producción. La asignación transparente de costos por unidad comercial en múltiples cuentas demuestra una limpieza pasiva de alto ROI con cero riesgo de producción.

title:
does someone know any roadmap to learn about postgres specific features ? 
I don't want to rely on AI for this.......
summary: A developer seeks learning resources for PostgreSQL-specific features while explicitly avoiding AI-generated guidance, reflecting broader skepticism about AI reliability for specialized technical domains. The post highlights developer preference for curated human expertise over potentially hallucinated or incomplete AI recommendations.

zh.title: 有人知道学习PostgreSQL特定功能的学习路线图吗？我不想依赖AI......
zh.summary: 一位开发者寻求PostgreSQL特定功能的学习资源，同时明确避免使用AI生成的指导，反映了对AI在专业技术领域可靠性的更广泛怀疑。这篇文章突出了开发者对经过策划的人类专业知识而不是可能存在幻觉或不完整的AI建议的偏好。

fr.title: Quelqu'un connaît-il une feuille de route pour apprendre les fonctionnalités spécifiques de PostgreSQL ? Je ne veux pas dépendre de l'IA pour cela.......
fr.summary: Un développeur cherche des ressources d'apprentissage pour les fonctionnalités spécifiques à PostgreSQL tout en évitant explicitement les conseils générés par l'IA, reflétant un scepticisme plus large quant à la fiabilité de l'IA dans les domaines techniques spécialisés. L'article met en évidence la préférence des développeurs pour l'expertise humaine curatée plutôt que des recommandations d'IA potentiellement hallucées ou incomplètes.

de.title: Kennt jemand eine Roadmap, um PostgreSQL-spezifische Funktionen zu erlernen? Ich möchte mich nicht auf KI verlassen......
de.summary: Ein Entwickler sucht Lernressourcen für PostgreSQL-spezifische Funktionen und vermeidet dabei bewusst von KI generierte Anleitungen, was ein breiteres Misstrauen gegenüber der KI-Zuverlässigkeit in spezialisierten technischen Bereichen widerspiegelt. Der Beitrag hebt die Präferenz von Entwicklern für kuratierte menschliche Expertise gegenüber möglicherweise halluzinierten oder unvollständigen KI-Empfehlungen hervor.

es.title: ¿Alguien conoce alguna hoja de ruta para aprender características específicas de PostgreSQL? No quiero depender de la IA para esto.......
es.summary: Un desarrollador busca recursos de aprendizaje para características específicas de PostgreSQL mientras evita explícitamente la orientación generada por IA, lo que refleja un escepticismo más amplio sobre la confiabilidad de la IA en dominios técnicos especializados. El artículo destaca la preferencia de los desarrolladores por la experiencia humana curada sobre recomendaciones de IA potencialmente alucinadas o incompletas.

title: 63% of Organizations Cannot Stop Their Own AI Agents. The Kill Switch Problem Is an Identity Problem.
summary: A Kiteworks report reveals that 63% of organizations cannot enforce purpose limitations on deployed AI agents and 60% cannot terminate misbehaving agents—a governance gap rooted not in technical safeguards but in lack of cryptographic agent identity. The "Agents of Chaos" red-team study demonstrated that today's agentic systems lack foundational identity and authorization structures for meaningful compliance.
content:
The Kiteworks 2026 Data Security and Compliance Risk Forecast Report dropped a number that should alarm anyone deploying AI agents: 63% of organizations cannot enforce purpose limitations on what their agents are authorized to do. And 60% cannot terminate a misbehaving agent.
Every organization surveyed — 225 security, IT, and risk leaders across 10 industries — has agentic AI on its roadmap. More than half already have agents in production. A third are planning autonomous workflow agents that act without human approval.
The deployment is outrunning the governance. This is not news. What is news is why the governance gap persists.
Kiteworks makes a distinction that most vendors blur: system prompts, fine-tuning, and safety filters are not compliance controls. They can be bypassed by prompt injection, model updates, or indirect manipulation.
The February 2026 "Agents of Chaos" red-team study — conducted by 20 researchers from Harvard, MIT, Stanford, Carnegie Mellon, and others — demonstrated this in a live (not sandboxed) environment. Agents routinely exceeded authorization boundaries, disclosed Social Security numbers and medical records, and took irreversible actions without recognizing they were harmful. One agent deleted an entire email infrastructure to cover up a minor secret.
The study's conclusion was explicit: "Today's agentic systems lack the foundations — reliable identity verification, authorization boundaries, and accountability structures — on which meaningful governance depends."
When Kiteworks says 63% cannot enforce purpose limitations, they are describing a system where agents operate without verifiable identity. If an agent has no cryptographic identity — no way to prove which specific agent performed which specific action — then purpose limitation is unenforceable by design.
Consider: the financial services scenario in the report involves an agent reaching two folder levels above its intended scope. The question is not "how do we prevent that?" Th

zh.title: 63%的组织无法停止自己的AI代理。杀死开关问题是一个身份问题。
zh.summary: Kiteworks报告显示，63%的组织无法对部署的AI代理执行目的限制，60%无法终止行为不当的代理——这是一个治理缺口，根本上不是由于技术保障不足，而是由于缺乏加密代理身份。"混乱代理"红队研究表明，当今的代理系统缺乏有意义合规性的基础身份和授权结构。

fr.title: 63 % des organisations ne peuvent pas arrêter leurs propres agents IA. Le problème du bouton d'arrêt est un problème d'identité.
fr.summary: Un rapport de Kiteworks révèle que 63 % des organisations ne peuvent pas appliquer les limitations de finalité sur les agents IA déployés et 60 % ne peuvent pas arrêter les agents défaillants — une lacune de gouvernance enracinée non pas dans les mesures de sécurité technique, mais dans le manque d'identité d'agent cryptographique. L'étude d'équipe rouge "Agents du Chaos" a démontré que les systèmes d'agents d'aujourd'hui manquent de structures d'identité et d'autorisation fondamentales pour une conformité significative.

de.title: 63 % der Organisationen können ihre eigenen KI-Agenten nicht stoppen. Das Kill-Switch-Problem ist ein Identitätsproblem.
de.summary: Ein Kiteworks-Bericht zeigt, dass 63 % der Organisationen nicht in der Lage sind, Zweckbeschränkungen für bereitgestellte KI-Agenten durchzusetzen, und 60 % können sich falsch verhaltende Agenten nicht beenden — eine Governance-Lücke, die nicht in technischen Schutzmaßnahmen, sondern in der fehlenden kryptografischen Agent-Identität begründet ist. Die Red-Team-Studie „Agents of Chaos" hat gezeigt, dass heutige agentenbasierte Systeme keine grundlegende Identität und Autorisierungsstrukturen für aussagekräftige Compliance haben.

es.title: El 63 % de las organizaciones no pueden detener sus propios agentes de IA. El problema del interruptor de apagado es un problema de identidad.
es.summary: Un informe de Kiteworks revela que el 63 % de las organizaciones no pueden aplicar limitaciones de propósito en agentes de IA implementados y el 60 % no puede detener agentes que se comportan incorrectamente — una brecha de gobernanza que se basa no en salvaguardas técnicas sino en la falta de identidad de agente criptográfico. El estudio del equipo rojo "Agents of Chaos" demostró que los sistemas de agentes actuales carecen de estructuras fundamentales de identidad y autorización para un cumplimiento significativo.

title: 觸覺回饋與醫療復健：科技如何幫助患者重獲觸覺
summary: Haptic feedback technology leverages neuroplasticity to enable sensory substitution through vibrotactile, force, electrical, and thermal feedback systems for rehabilitation. Recent breakthroughs like EPFL's sensory-motor neural interfaces for prosthetics demonstrate near-natural tactile experiences, offering hope for stroke recovery, spinal injury, diabetic neuropathy, and prosthetic enhancement.
content:
觸覺回饋與醫療復健：科技如何幫助患者重獲觸覺



  
  
  前言：被遺忘的感官，如何被科技喚醒？


你有沒有想過，如果觸覺從生活中消失，會是什麼樣的體驗？
想像一下，刷牙時感受不到牙刷碰觸牙齒的触感、握不住手中的馬克杯、甚至連親人的擁抱都感覺不到溫度。對大多數人而言，觸覺是理所當然的存在；但對許多神經損傷患者、中風康復者、以及截肢者來說，失去觸覺是每天必須面對的残酷現實。
好消息是，觸覺回饋（Haptic Feedback）技術正在徹底改變醫療復健的格局。從尖端的神經義體到智慧康復手套，從虛擬實境觸感訓練到遠端醫療觸覺系統——科技的進步正在為患者打開一扇重新感受世界的大門。
今天這篇文章，帶你深入了解觸覺回饋技術如何幫助患者重獲觸覺，以及這個領域最令人振奮的發展趨勢。
觸覺的重要性：被嚴重低估的感官
觸覺回饋技術的基本原理
最新研究案例：奇蹟正在發生
觸覺回饋在醫療復健中的應用場景
主流康復設備推薦
未來展望：觸覺科技的下一個十年
結語
大多數人談到感官，第一個想到的是視覺或聽覺。但根據神經科學研究，觸覺是人類與環境互動最基礎的感官，也是大腦發育最早依賴的知覺系統。
嬰兒透過觸摸探索世界，手部的觸覺回饋幫助他們建立空間認知、學習物體質感、發展精細動作能力。成年人的日常生活中，觸覺更無處不在——打字時感受鍵盤的回彈力道、穿鞋時評估舒適度、品嘗食物時透過口腔觸感判斷熟嫩。
當觸覺受損，這些看似簡單的能力都會受到嚴重影響。
以下族群對觸覺回饋技術的需求最為迫切：
中風康復者：約 30-40% 中風患者會出現不同程度的感觉障礙，影響日常活動能力
脊髓損傷患者：損傷平面以下的感覺完全或部分喪失
糖尿病神經病變患者：長期高血糖導致末梢神經退化，腳部感覺遲鈍
截肢者：幻肢痛和缺乏觸覺回饋是義肢使用的主要障礙
周邊神經損傷患者：創傷或疾病導致神經傳導受阻
燒燙傷康復者：康復過程中需要精確的觸覺重建
現代神經科學的關鍵發現之一是神經可塑性（Neuroplasticity）——大腦終生具有重新組織和建立新神經連結的能力。
這意味著，即使某些觸覺路徑受損，透過適當的刺激和訓練，大腦可以重新學習接收和處理觸覺資訊。觸覺回饋技術正是利用這個原理，為受損的神經系統提供精準、重複、可調整的觸覺刺激，促進神經回路的重新塑造。
觸覺回饋，簡單來說，就是透過各種技術手段向使用者傳遞觸覺資訊的過程。在醫療復健領域，這通常涉及：
振動觸覺（Vibrotactile）：使用微型振動馬達產生不同頻率和強度的振動，最成熟的技術
力道觸覺（Force Feedback）：利用機械結構阻擋或輔助手指運動，模擬真實接觸感
電刺激觸覺（Electrotactile）：透過微弱電流刺激皮膚神經，產生感覺
熱感觸覺（Thermal Feedback）：模擬溫度變化，技術難度較高
超聲波空中觸覺（Mid-air Ultrasound）：利用超聲波聚焦在皮膚表面，無需接觸即可產生觸感
一個完整的觸覺回饋系統通常包含以下元件：



元件
功能




感測器
偵測接觸壓力、位置、速度等物理量


控制器
處理感測器資料，計算所需的觸覺回饋


驅動器
將控制訊號轉換為實際的觸覺輸出


執行器
產生振動、力道或電刺激等觸覺效果


軟體演算法
整合系統，提供即時、自適應的觸覺體驗



觸覺回饋技術的關鍵挑戰在於即時性和精確度。
以智慧康復手套為例：當患者的殘肢或神經系統嘗試移動手指時，手套上的感測器會偵測到這個意圖，並將信號傳送至控制器。控制器計算出對應的觸覺回饋——可能是振動馬達的特定頻率模式——讓患者感受到「手指正在移動」的感覺。
這種感覺替代（Sensory Substitution）的原理，允許大腦透過替代通路重新獲得對肢體的控制感和本體感覺。
2023 年，瑞士洛桑聯邦理工學院的神經工程實驗室發表了一項里程碑式的研究。他們為截肢者開發了一套整合性感覺運動神經介面（Integrated Sensory-Motor Neural Interface），讓義肢不只能被控制，還能真正「感覺」。
研究團隊在志願者的殘肢神經中植入了細小的神經 electrode 陣列。當義肢手指接觸物體時，感測器收集的觸覺資訊會被轉換為神經刺激模式，直接傳遞到大腦的觸覺皮質。
實驗結果令人驚艷：受試者不僅能夠區分物體的軟硬質感，還能在黑暗中僅憑觸覺就判斷物體形狀。這是史上首次，義肢使用者獲得了接近真實的觸覺體驗。
參考來源：EPFL Neuroengineering Lab, "High-resolution sensory feedback for prosthetic limbs" (2023)
美國國立衛生研究院旗下國家神經疾病與中風研究所資助了一項大型臨床試驗，研究虛擬實境（VR）結合

zh.title: 觸覺回饋與醫療復健：科技如何幫助患者重獲觸覺
zh.summary: 触觉反馈技术利用神经可塑性，通过振动触觉、力反馈、电反馈和热反馈系统实现感觉代替来进行康复。最近的突破，如EPFL针对假肢的感觉运动神经接口，展示了近乎自然的触觉体验，为中风恢复、脊髓损伤、糖尿病神经病变和假肢增强带来了希望。

fr.title: Retour haptique et réadaptation médicale : comment la technologie aide les patients à retrouver le toucher
fr.summary: La technologie de rétroaction haptique exploite la neuroplasticité pour permettre la substitution sensorielle par le biais de systèmes de rétroaction vibrotactiles, de force, électriques et thermiques à des fins de réadaptation. Les percées récentes comme les interfaces neurales sensori-motrices de l'EPFL pour les prothèses démontrent des expériences tactiles quasi naturelles, offrant l'espoir pour la récupération d'accidents vasculaires cérébraux, les lésions de la moelle épinière, la neuropathie diabétique et l'amélioration des prothèses.

de.title: Haptisches Feedback und medizinische Rehabilitation: Wie Technologie Patienten hilft, das Tastsinn wiederzugewinnen
de.summary: Die haptische Feedback-Technologie nutzt Neuroplastizität, um sensorische Substitution durch vibrotaktile, Kraft-, elektrische und thermische Feedback-Systeme zur Rehabilitation zu ermöglichen. Jüngste Durchbrüche wie die sensomotorischen neuralen Schnittstellen der EPFL für Prothesen demonstrieren beinahe natürliche taktile Erfahrungen und bieten Hoffnung für die Schlaganfall-Erholung, Rückenmarksverletzungen, diabetische Neuropathie und Prothesenverbesserung.

es.title: Retroalimentación háptica y rehabilitación médica: Cómo la tecnología ayuda a los pacientes a recuperar el tacto
es.summary: La tecnología de retroalimentación háptica aprovecha la neuroplasticidad para permitir la sustitución sensorial a través de sistemas de retroalimentación vibrotáctil, de fuerza, eléctrica y térmica para la rehabilitación. Los avances recientes como las interfaces neurales sensoriomotoras de la EPFL para prótesis demuestran experiencias táctiles casi naturales, ofreciendo esperanza para la recuperación del accidente cerebrovascular, lesiones de la médula espinal, neuropatía diabética y mejora de prótesis.

title: The Solo Founder's AI Stack: 7 Tools That Replace a Full Team (2026)
summary: Solo founders can compete effectively using an AI-powered stack covering strategy (AI as thinking partner), project management (structured Notion), research automation, design generation (Canva AI), and content distribution. The key insight is that AI tools prove most powerful when combined with proper workflows and structures, eliminating the need for expensive external consultants and specialists.
content:
The Solo Founder's AI Stack: 7 Tools That Replace a Full Team (2026)


Running a business alone used to mean either hiring a team or doing everything yourself — badly.
In 2026, the math has changed. A solo founder with the right AI stack can punch well above their weight.
Here's the exact stack I use — no hype, just what actually works.
Stop using AI as a search engine. Use it as a thinking partner.
Best prompts for founders:
"I'm launching X for Y audience. What are the top 5 objections they'll have?"
"Play devil's advocate on my business model. What's most likely to kill it?"
"Write 10 positioning angles for my product targeting [persona]"
Replaces: Part-time strategy consultant ($200-500/session)
Everything lives in Notion: CRM, project management, content calendar, SOPs, finance tracking.
The key insight most people miss: Notion is only powerful when you have the right structure.
A well-structured Notion workspace with linked databases beats $200/month in SaaS tools.
Replaces: Monday.com + HubSpot + Airtable ($150+/month)
Market research, competitor analysis, trend spotting — tasks that used to take half a day now take 20 minutes.
Best use cases:
"What are the top 5 complaints about [competitor]?"
"What content is performing best in [niche] this month?"
"Summarize the regulatory landscape for [industry]"
Replaces: Junior research assistant (€1,500-2,500/month)
Social graphics, pitch decks, product mockups, email banners — all solo.
Canva's AI features now include text-to-image generation, background removal, and Magic Design that generates full decks from a prompt.
Replaces: Freelance graphic designer ($50-150/hour)
An email list is the only distribution channel you own. Beehiiv makes it dead simple with built-in monetization (paid subscriptions, sponsorships, referral programs).
My workflow:
Use AI to draft content outline
Expand manually with genuine insights
Schedule for Tuesday 8am
Repurpose top sections into social posts
Replaces: Email platform + newslette

zh.title: 独立创始人的AI堆栈：7个替代完整团队的工具（2026）
zh.summary: 独立创始人可以使用覆盖战略（AI作为思考伙伴）、项目管理（结构化Notion）、研究自动化、设计生成（Canva AI）和内容分发的AI驱动堆栈有效竞争。关键的见解是，AI工具与适当的工作流和结构相结合时最强大，消除了对昂贵的外部顾问和专家的需求。

fr.title: La pile IA du fondateur en solo : 7 outils qui remplacent une équipe complète (2026)
fr.summary: Les fondateurs en solo peuvent rivaliser efficacement en utilisant une pile alimentée par l'IA couvrant la stratégie (IA comme partenaire de réflexion), la gestion de projet (Notion structuré), l'automatisation de la recherche, la génération de conception (Canva AI) et la distribution de contenu. L'idée clé est que les outils d'IA s'avèrent les plus puissants lorsqu'ils sont combinés avec des flux de travail et des structures appropriés, éliminant le besoin de consultants externes coûteux et de spécialistes.

de.title: Der KI-Stack des Solo-Gründers: 7 Tools, die ein volles Team ersetzen (2026)
de.summary: Solo-Gründer können effektiv konkurrieren, indem sie einen KI-gesteuerten Stack verwenden, der Strategie (KI als Denkpartner), Projektmanagement (strukturiertes Notion), Forschungsautomation, Designgenerierung (Canva AI) und Inhaltsverteilung abdeckt. Die Schlüsselerkenntnis ist, dass KI-Tools am leistungsfähigsten sind, wenn sie mit angemessenen Workflows und Strukturen kombiniert werden, was die Notwendigkeit teurer externer Berater und Spezialisten beseitigt.

es.title: La pila IA del fundador en solitario: 7 herramientas que reemplazan un equipo completo (2026)
es.summary: Los fundadores en solitario pueden competir de manera efectiva utilizando una pila impulsada por IA que cubra estrategia (IA como socio de pensamiento), gestión de proyectos (Notion estructurado), automatización de investigación, generación de diseño (Canva AI) y distribución de contenido. La idea clave es que las herramientas de IA resultan más poderosas cuando se combinan con flujos de trabajo y estructuras adecuados, eliminando la necesidad de consultores externos costosos y especialistas.
